**Notodden** ([pronunciation](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/transcoded/e/e0/Notodden.ogg/Notodden.ogg.mp3 "Play audio")[ⓘ](/wiki/File:Notodden.ogg "File:Notodden.ogg")) is a [municipality](/wiki/List_of_municipalities_of_Norway "List of municipalities of Norway") in [Telemark](/wiki/Telemark "Telemark") county, [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"). It is located in the [traditional district](/wiki/Traditional_districts_of_Norway "Traditional districts of Norway") of [Aust-Telemark](/wiki/Aust-Telemark "Aust-Telemark"). The [administrative centre](/wiki/Administrative_centre "Administrative centre") of the municipality is the [town of Notodden](/wiki/Notodden_(town) "Notodden (town)"). Other population centres include the villages of [Bolkesjø](/wiki/Bolkesj%C3%B8 "Bolkesjø"), [Gransherad](/wiki/Gransherad_(village) "Gransherad (village)"), [Heddal](/wiki/Heddal_(village) "Heddal (village)"), [Hjuksebø](/wiki/Hjukseb%C3%B8 "Hjuksebø"), [Hjuksevelta](/wiki/Hjuksevelta "Hjuksevelta"), [Rudsgrendi](/wiki/Rudsgrendi "Rudsgrendi"), [Tinnoset](/wiki/Tinnoset "Tinnoset"), and [Yli](/wiki/Yli "Yli").[[4]](#cite_note-snl-4)

The 984-square-kilometre (380 sq mi) municipality is the 118th largest by area out of the 356 municipalities in Norway. Notodden is the 94th most populous municipality in Norway with a population of 13,025. The municipality's [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") is 14.3 inhabitants per square kilometre (37/sq mi) and its population has increased by 4.2% over the previous 10-year period.[[5]](#cite_note-ssb_pop-5)[[6]](#cite_note-ssb_area-6)

Norway's largest [stave church](/wiki/Stave_church "Stave church"), [Heddal Stave Church](/wiki/Heddal_Stave_Church "Heddal Stave Church"), is located in [Heddal](/wiki/Heddal_(village) "Heddal (village)"), a few kilometres west the municipal centre. [Notodden Airport](/wiki/Notodden_Airport "Notodden Airport"), is located west of the town of Notodden, along the [European route E134](/wiki/European_route_E134 "European route E134") highway. [Norsk Hydro](/wiki/Norsk_Hydro "Norsk Hydro") was founded in this town. Notodden is well known for the annual [Notodden Blues Festival](/wiki/Notodden_Blues_Festival "Notodden Blues Festival"), which is considered one of the best blues festivals in Europe. It also has a well-known metal festival called *Motstøy Festivalen*. The [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") club [Notodden FK](/wiki/Notodden_FK "Notodden FK") is headquartered here.

## General information

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Notodden&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: General information")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5c/Rjukan-Notodden_Industrial_Heritage_Site.jpg/250px-Rjukan-Notodden_Industrial_Heritage_Site.jpg)](/wiki/File:Rjukan-Notodden_Industrial_Heritage_Site.jpg)

Industrial heritage in Notodden

On 1 January 1913, the newly-designated [kjøpstad](/wiki/Kj%C3%B8pstad "Kjøpstad") (town) of [Notodden](/wiki/Notodden_(town) "Notodden (town)") (population: 4,821) was separated from [Heddal Municipality](/wiki/Heddal "Heddal") to form a separate self-governing town as an [enclave](/wiki/Enclave "Enclave") within Heddal Municipality. This left Heddal with 2,890 residents. During the 1960s, there were many municipal mergers across Norway due to the work of the [Schei Committee](/wiki/Schei_Committee "Schei Committee"). On 1 January 1964, Heddal Municipality was dissolved and the following areas were merged to form a new, larger Notodden Municipality:[[7]](#cite_note-Dag-7)

* most of [Gransherad Municipality](/wiki/Gransherad "Gransherad") (population: 1,115) – except for the upper [Jondalen](/wiki/Jondalen "Jondalen") valley which became part of [Kongsberg Municipality](/wiki/Kongsberg_Municipality "Kongsberg Municipality")
* all of [Heddal Municipality](/wiki/Heddal "Heddal") (population: 4,844)
* the [Rudsgrendi](/wiki/Rudsgrendi "Rudsgrendi") area of [Hovin Municipality](/wiki/Hovin,_Telemark "Hovin, Telemark") (population: 21)
* the [town of Notodden](/wiki/Notodden_(town) "Notodden (town)") (population: 7,523)

### Name

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Notodden&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Name")]

The municipality (originally the town) is named after a small [cotter](/wiki/Cotter_(farmer) "Cotter (farmer)")'s farmstead (*husmannsplass*) located on the old *Tinne* farm since this was the original site of the town (near the mouth of the river [Tinnelva](/wiki/Tinnelva "Tinnelva") at the lake [Heddalsvatnet](/wiki/Heddalsvatnet "Heddalsvatnet")). The first element is derived from the word *[nót](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/n%C3%B3t#Old_Norse "wikt:nót")* which means "to [fish using a seine](/wiki/Seine_fishing "Seine fishing")". The last element is the definite singular form of *[odde](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/odde#Norwegian "wikt:odde")* which means "[headland](/wiki/Headland "Headland")". Thus it is a "headland for net fishing".[[4]](#cite_note-snl-4)

### Coat of arms

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Notodden&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Coat of arms")]

The [coat of arms](/wiki/Coat_of_arms "Coat of arms") was granted on 11 August 1939. The official [blazon](/wiki/Blazon "Blazon") is *"[Azure](/wiki/Azure_(heraldry) "Azure (heraldry)"), a [pale](/wiki/Pale_(heraldry) "Pale (heraldry)") wavy and four lightning bolts [in saltire](/wiki/Saltire "Saltire") [argent](/wiki/Argent "Argent")"* ([Norwegian](/wiki/Norwegian_language "Norwegian language"): *På blå bunn en loddrett sølvelv som sender lynstråler av sølv til begge sider*). This means the arms have a blue [field](/wiki/Field_(heraldry) "Field (heraldry)") (background) and the [charge](/wiki/Charge_(heraldry) "Charge (heraldry)") is a wavy vertical bar and four [lightning](/wiki/Lightning "Lightning") bolts emanating from the centre and making an X-shape. The charge has a [tincture](/wiki/Tincture_(heraldry) "Tincture (heraldry)") of [argent](/wiki/Argent "Argent") which means it is commonly colored white, but if it is made out of metal, then silver is used. The design symbolizes that Notodden was created by the exploitation of [hydroelectric power](/wiki/Hydroelectricity "Hydroelectricity") on the river [Tinnelva](/wiki/Tinnelva "Tinnelva") when [Norsk Hydro](/wiki/Norsk_Hydro "Norsk Hydro") started its operations with saltpeter production and power development in 1905. The vertical bar represents the Tinnfoss [waterfall](/wiki/Waterfall "Waterfall") along the river and the lightning bolts represent electricity. The arms were designed by Jens Høibø. The municipal flag has the same design as the coat of arms.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)

### Churches

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Notodden&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Churches")]

The [Church of Norway](/wiki/Church_of_Norway "Church of Norway") has four parishes (*sokn*) within the municipality of Notodden. It is part of the [Øvre Telemark prosti](/wiki/%C3%98vre_Telemark_prosti "Øvre Telemark prosti") ([deanery](/wiki/Deanery "Deanery")) in the [Diocese of Agder og Telemark](/wiki/Diocese_of_Agder_og_Telemark "Diocese of Agder og Telemark").

## Government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Notodden&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Government")]

Notodden Municipality is responsible for [primary education](/wiki/Primary_education "Primary education") (through 10th grade), outpatient [health services](/wiki/Health_care "Health care"), [senior citizen](/wiki/Old_age "Old age") services, [welfare](/wiki/Welfare_spending "Welfare spending") and other [social services](/wiki/Social_work "Social work"), [zoning](/wiki/Zoning "Zoning"), [economic development](/wiki/Economic_development "Economic development"), and municipal [roads](/wiki/Road "Road") and utilities. The municipality is governed by a [municipal council](/wiki/Municipal_council_(Norway) "Municipal council (Norway)") of [directly elected](/wiki/Direct_election "Direct election") representatives. The [mayor](/wiki/Mayor "Mayor") is [indirectly elected](/wiki/Indirect_election "Indirect election") by a vote of the municipal council.[[10]](#cite_note-ks-10) The municipality is under the jurisdiction of the [Telemark District Court](/wiki/Telemark_District_Court "Telemark District Court") and the [Agder Court of Appeal](/wiki/Agder_Court_of_Appeal "Agder Court of Appeal").

### Municipal council

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Notodden&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Municipal council")]

The [municipal council](/wiki/Municipal_council_(Norway) "Municipal council (Norway)") *(Kommunestyre)* of Notodden is made up of 41 representatives that are elected to four-year terms. ## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Notodden&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Geography")]

The municipality is located in [Aust-Telemark](/wiki/Aust-Telemark "Aust-Telemark"), along the border with [Buskerud](/wiki/Buskerud "Buskerud"). Notodden Municipality includes the lands between the lakes [Tinnsjå](/wiki/Tinnsj%C3%A5 "Tinnsjå") and [Heddalsvatnet](/wiki/Heddalsvatnet "Heddalsvatnet") lake. The rivers [Tinnelva](/wiki/Tinnelva "Tinnelva") and [Heddøla](/wiki/Hedd%C3%B8la "Heddøla") both run through the municipality. The [Blefjell](/wiki/Blefjell "Blefjell") mountains are located in the northern part of the municipality.

## Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Notodden&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Climate")]

Notodden has a [humid continental climate](/wiki/Humid_continental_climate "Humid continental climate") (Dfb), with cold winters and mild summers. Situated inland at low altitude in the Telemark region, it is one of the warmer towns in Norway during summer. The weather station at [Notodden Airport](/wiki/Notodden_Airport "Notodden Airport") has been recording since March 1970 (temperature and wind speed). Precipitation data is from a different station in Notodden. The all-time high temperature 33.3 °C (91.9 °F) was recorded on 27 July 2018.

## Twin towns

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Notodden&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Twin towns")]

Main article: [List of twin towns and sister cities in Norway](/wiki/List_of_twin_towns_and_sister_cities_in_Norway "List of twin towns and sister cities in Norway")

In 2008, the Notodden council decided to end the twin city agreements with Ilisalmi, Nyköping, and Stelle. At the same time Suwałki was approved as a new twin city.[[41]](#cite_note-41)[[42]](#cite_note-42)
